review_metadata.prior_review: 2026-04-03 (initial review found stale broader counts at 429/30; those have since been updated to 748/49 in paper but new discrepancies found)
sources_checked: lake build output (zero sorry confirmed, 8076 jobs)

executive_summary: Core formal verification claims are solid: zero sorry confirmed by lake build, all cited Lean theorems exist, 14 Aristotle proofs verified against 4 run IDs, master synthesis is a genuine five-part conjunction, 3 axioms confirmed. The primary issues are (1) theorem count discrepancies under different counting conventions (55 claimed for chirality modules vs 54-58 actual, 748 claimed total vs 762 actual), (2) one Aristotle run ID misattribution, and (3) internal inconsistency between Discussion (588/38) and Conclusion (748/49) figures.

claims.location: Abstract line 51-52; Table I; Conclusion line 325
claims.detail: Actual counts by grepping ^(theorem|lemma|private lemma|instance): LH=34 (28 theorems + 1 lemma + 5 instances), GS=18 (14 theorems + 3 private lemmas + 1 instance), TPF=12 (12 theorems). Strict theorem-only count: 28+14+12=54. If the unnamed Module.Finite instance (fock_space_finite_dim) is counted as a theorem: 28+15+12=55. The 55 count is defensible only if: (a) the fock_space_finite_dim instance is counted, AND (b) the 3 private helper lemmas in GS are NOT counted. This is an unusual convention. Prior review accepted 55 under this reasoning.
claims.recommendation: Add footnote: 'counting the fock_space_finite_dim instance and excluding 3 private helper lemmas'

claims.detail: All three files exist at lean/SKEFTHawking/.

lake build completes with 'Build completed successfully (8076 jobs)' and only lint warnings (unused simp args in GTCommutation.lean). Grep for \bsorry\b across all .lean files finds only comments/docstrings, never active proof terms.

claims.location: Abstract lines 54-55; Table I; Acknowledgments lines 347-349
claims.detail: ARISTOTLE_THEOREMS in constants.py confirms 14 entries across 4 chirality run IDs: run_20260328_132925 (7), run_20260328_142342 (3), run_20260328_151228 (3), run_20260328_170451 (1). Total: 7+3+3+1=14.

Grep for ^(theorem|lemma|private lemma|noncomputable instance|instance ) across 49 .lean files yields 762 declarations across 48 files (Basic.lean has 0). Module count 49 is correct. Axiom count 3 is correct (non_abelian_center_discrete, gs_nogo_axiom, z16_classification). Theorem count 748 is 14 fewer than actual 762. No consistent counting convention produces exactly 748.
claims.recommendation: Recount with explicit convention. If excluding unnamed instances (5 in LH + others), document the convention.

claims.detail: ls lean/SKEFTHawking/*.lean | wc -l = 49
constants.py line 1095: assert ARISTOTLE_PROVED_COUNT == 252. Distinct non-manual run IDs: 34 (counted manually from registry). Both 252 and 30+ are accurate.
28 theorem declarations (excluding 1 lemma two_pi_pos and 5 instances). 7 Aristotle entries confirmed in constants.py.

14 theorem decls + 3 private lemmas + 1 instance + 1 axiom. Paper's '15' requires counting the unnamed Module.Finite instance but NOT the 3 private helper lemmas. Unusual but defensible. Aristotle count 7 is correct (3+3+1 from 3 runs). Axiom count 1 correct (gs_nogo_axiom).
Exact match. 12 theorems, all manual (norm_num, rfl, or reexports). Zero in ARISTOTLE_THEOREMS.

claims.claim: BrillouinZone d := Fin d -> AddCircle (2pi), proved compact via Pi.compactSpace + AddCircle.compact
claims.detail: LatticeHamiltonian.lean line 68: BrillouinZone d = Fin d -> AddCircle (2*Real.pi). Line 85: brillouin_zone_compact uses Pi.compactSpace.

claims.claim: FermionicFockSpace k := ExteriorAlgebra C (Fin k -> C), fock_space_finite_dim via Aristotle run_20260328_170451 using 3 helper lemmas
claims.detail: GoltermanShamir.lean line 82: abbrev FermionicFockSpace k := ExteriorAlgebra C (Fin k -> C). Line 262: Module.Finite instance. Three helper lemmas at lines 188, 199, 252. constants.py line 888: fock_space_finite_dim -> run_20260328_170451.
GoltermanShamir.lean line 448: theorem c1_implies_i2. constants.py line 880: run_20260328_142342.
LatticeHamiltonian.lean line 304. constants.py line 871: run_20260328_132925.
LatticeHamiltonian.lean line 332. constants.py line 872: run_20260328_132925.

claims.claim: tpf_violates_C2 and tpf_bosonic_exceeds_fock via Aristotle run_20260328_142342
claims.detail: tpf_violates_C2 IS in run_20260328_142342 (constants.py line 878). But tpf_bosonic_exceeds_fock is in run_20260328_151228 (constants.py line 885), NOT run_20260328_142342 as claimed. The paper incorrectly attributes tpf_bosonic_exceeds_fock to the wrong run.
claims.recommendation: Change to 'Aristotle runs run_20260328_142342 and run_20260328_151228' or cite the correct run for each theorem.

claims.detail: constants.py line 873: integers_not_finite -> run_20260328_132925.

claims.claim: tpf_outside_gs_scope_main is a machine-checked five-part conjunction
claims.detail: TPFEvasion.lean lines 149-156: five-part conjunction (not_FiniteDimensional, not_Finite_Z, not_ContinuousAt_round, 4+1=5, 9-3<9). Paper's pseudocode uses abbreviated names (violation_I3 etc.) but structure matches exactly.

CLAUDE.md confirms Mathlib pinned to 8f9d9cff. Prior review verified lean-toolchain says v4.28.0 and lakefile.toml references 8f9d9cff. Not independently re-verified in this review (no lakefile read).

Internal inconsistency: Discussion says 588/38, Conclusion says 748/49. These refer to different snapshots (pre-Phase 5a vs current). Current codebase has 762/49. The 588 figure was accurate at the time of original drafting (per prior review) but conflicts with the 748 in the same paper.
claims.recommendation: Either pin both figures to the same snapshot or update Discussion to match Conclusion (748/49).

claims.claim: Phase 5 adds 372 theorems across 22 modules, bringing total to 588 + 2 axioms
claims.detail: Historical snapshot claim. Conflicts with Conclusion's 748/49. Not verifiable against current codebase which has grown beyond 588.

claims.claim: Nine GS conditions (6 explicit + 3 implicit), 7 substantive, TPF violates 5
claims.detail: LatticeHamiltonian.lean: gs_explicit_count=6, gs_implicit_count=3, gs_total_conditions=9. GoltermanShamir.lean: substantive_condition_count: 7+2=9. TPFEvasion.lean: potential_violation_count: 3+2=5.

claims.claim: Companion paper extends to 748 theorems across 49 modules
claims.detail: Same as C05. Actual count is 762 theorem-like declarations, not 748. Module count 49 is correct.

claims.claim: 55 theorems + 1 axiom across three Lean 4 modules, with zero sorry
claims.detail: Repeats C01 (55 defensible with noted convention), C02 (3 modules confirmed), C03 (zero sorry confirmed).
Three axioms found: non_abelian_center_discrete (GaugeErasure.lean), gs_nogo_axiom (GoltermanShamir.lean), z16_classification (Z16Classification.lean).

claims.claim: fig53, fig54, fig60, fig61 referenced in paper exist
claims.detail: All four PNG files present in papers/paper7_chirality_formal/figures/.

claims.claim: First-ever definitions of pivotal, spherical, fusion categories + Drinfeld double D(G) + gauge emergence theorem in a proof assistant
claims.detail: Qualitative priority claim. The Lean files exist (KLinearCategory.lean, SphericalCategory.lean, FusionCategory.lean, DrinfeldDouble.lean, GaugeEmergence.lean). 'First-ever' cannot be verified from codebase alone.

claims.claim: First formal verification of lattice chirality in a proof assistant
claims.detail: Priority claim. Formalization exists and is machine-checked. Cannot verify 'first' without literature survey.

claims.claim: Bosonic rotor ancillas L^2(S^1) are not fermionic, proved by tpf_violates_C2
claims.detail: GoltermanShamir.lean line 465: theorem tpf_violates_C2 proves existence of N > c.local_dim from TPF's unbounded dimension.

claims.claim: Aristotle filled 14 of the 55 sorry gaps in the chirality modules including fock_space_finite_dim
claims.detail: 14 Aristotle entries confirmed for chirality modules. fock_space_finite_dim specifically via run_20260328_170451.

failures.issue: Total theorem count 748 does not match actual 762 (14 discrepancy). No consistent counting convention found to produce exactly 748.
tpf_bosonic_exceeds_fock attributed to run_20260328_142342 but constants.py shows run_20260328_151228.

warnings.issue: 55 chirality theorem count requires unusual convention (count unnamed instance, exclude private lemmas). Defensible but should be documented.
Internal inconsistency: Discussion says 588/38, Conclusion says 748/49. Different snapshots in same paper.
'First-ever' priority claims unverifiable from codebase alone.

recommended_actions: Fix Aristotle run ID for tpf_bosonic_exceeds_fock on line 169 (change run_20260328_142342 to run_20260328_151228, or cite both runs)
Recount total theorems across all 49 modules with explicit convention; update 748 to actual count
Reconcile Discussion '588/38' with Conclusion '748/49' (update Discussion to current figures or add explicit snapshot notation)
Consider adding footnote explaining the 55-count convention (includes fock_space_finite_dim instance, excludes 3 private helper lemmas)
